perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in one counterpart which shall be deemed to be the sole original.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'any number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original' to 'one counterpart which shall be deemed to be the sole original' creates a contradiction. If other parts of the agreement, or standard legal practice, assume that multiple signed copies exist for each party, this restriction will create uncertainty about the valid form of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby confirm the agreement by and among them to the joint filing on behalf of them of the Statement on Schedule 13D and any and all further amendments thereto, with respect to the securities of the above referenced issuer, and that this Agreement be included as an Exhibit to such filing. This Agreement may be executed in one counterpart which shall be deemed to be the sole original and all of which together shall be deemed to constitute one and the same Agreement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of February 20, 2020. perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for multiple counterparts to be considered originals. The changed text limits the agreement to a single original counterpart. The contradicted text demonstrates a situation where multiple parties are signing the agreement, suggesting the need for multiple original counterparts. The change therefore directly contradicts the signing structure as it is shown in the contradicted text, which would necessitate multiple original counterparts, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this Agreement as of February 20, 2020.
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned may execute this Agreement at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original phrase 'hereby execute this Agreement' implies a mandatory action with a specific date. Changing it to 'may execute this Agreement at their discretion' makes the execution optional and removes the fixed date, creating a contradiction. The entire agreement's validity becomes questionable if execution is not guaranteed. If other sections rely on the guaranteed execution of the agreement, this change directly contradicts those assumptions.

perturbation.justification: The original text's mandatory execution ('hereby execute') is contradicted by the changed text's optional execution ('may execute at their discretion'). The Contradicted Text is literally the Changed Text, meaning that the parties signed a document that says they 'may' execute the agreement, which is a contradiction, as the parties already executed the agreement, and signed it. This is a strong contradiction regarding the agreement's validity and enforceability, as the entire agreement's validity is now uncertain.
